cards.title: Dutch Walking Festival Boosts Children's Health and Happiness
cards.body: Dutch kids participate in an annual four-night walking festival with parents and teachers to explore neighborhoods and build friendships. This tradition appears genuinely transformative for child development and wellbeing.

cards.title: Should Parents Open a Pension for Their Kids?
cards.body: Parents are being encouraged to set up self-invested personal pensions for children, though many families struggle with their own retirement savings. The advice raises questions about where funding should come from.

cards.title: AI is Changing How Parents Approach Caregiving
cards.body: Some mothers are using AI tools to support parenting decisions, though research shows women in heterosexual marriages still shoulder most caregiving duties. The trend reflects ongoing challenges in shared parental responsibility.

cards.title: How Parents Can Help De-Escalate Teen Outbursts
cards.body: Teenagers often become emotionally reactive and angry, leaving parents seeking practical strategies. Five evidence-based steps can help parents de-escalate these difficult moments.

cards.title: Supporting Grieving Youth on Father's Day
cards.body: Father's Day can be emotionally difficult for children who have lost a parent. Parents and caregivers can learn specific ways to support grieving children during this challenging holiday.

cards.title: Why Teenagers Hate Their Parents
cards.body: Teens view parents as adversaries when adults continue treating them like children instead of emerging adults. Understanding this developmental shift helps parents adjust their approach.

cards.title: Pathological Demand Avoidance in Children Explained
cards.body: Extreme refusal in children signals a need for curiosity rather than stricter consequences, though PDA remains a controversial diagnosis. Understanding the roots of resistance helps parents respond more effectively.

cards.title: Planning a Meaningful Summer with Your Kids
cards.body: Setting clear intentions now can help families experience the summer they truly want. The process is easier than many parents think.

cards.title: Helping Children Build Social Connection Skills
cards.body: Six-year-olds need strong social skills for successful school transitions, yet their unique backgrounds may create barriers. Targeted support helps children overcome these challenges.

cards.title: When Family Success Triggers Sabotage
cards.body: Family systems built on toxicity may respond to a child's success with criticism, guilt, or sabotage. Understanding crab mentality helps parents protect their children's achievements.

cards.title: Hungary's Experiment Reveals Birth Rate Patterns
cards.body: Hungary's pronatalist policies initially boosted births but then fell back, offering lessons for countries trying to increase fertility rates. The mixed results highlight the complexity of demographic challenges.

cards.title: UK Social Media Ban and Children's Digital Lives
cards.body: Apps including TikTok and Snapchat will be banned for UK teens starting early 2027, though impacts on gaming and YouTube remain unclear. The policy aims to protect younger users from digital harms.

cards.title: Prince George's Eton College Transition Coming
cards.body: Prince George will attend Eton College from September, following his father's path through the prestigious school. The announcement highlights typical educational decisions for elite families.
